title: Alert Check - BGP Neighbor Status -EQ Idle or Active
description: SWQL alert condition query for: NETWORK_N_P_AE: BGP Neighbor Status -EQ Idle or Active
code:
-- Alert: NETWORK_N_P_AE: BGP Neighbor Status -EQ Idle or Active
SELECT Neighbors.Uri, Neighbors.DisplayName FROM Orion.Routing.Neighbors AS Neighbors
join Orion.Nodes n ON Neighbors.NodeID=n.NodeID
join Orion.Pollers p ON n.NodeID=p.NetObjectID
join Orion.NodesCustomProperties ncp ON n.NodeID=ncp.NodeID
where n.UnManaged = False AND (ncp.Application_Code like 'NOEE' OR ncp.Application_Code like 'NOEC')
AND (p.PollerType like '%BGP%' AND p.Enabled = True)
AND (Neighbors.ProtocolID = 14)
AND (n.Caption like '$caption')

code:

Orion.Routing.Neighbors AS Neighbors join Orion.Nodes n ON Neighbors.NodeID=n.NodeID join Orion.Pollers p ON n.NodeID=p.NetObjectID

description: Automation - sql script/query
code:
set lines 130
col tablespace_name for a30
column sumb format 999,999,999
column extents format 9999
column bytes format 999,999,999
column largest format 999,999,999
column Tot_Size format 999,999,999,999
column Tot_Free format 999,999,999,999
column Pct_Free heading "PCTF" format 999
column Chunks_Free heading "CHKF" format 9999
column Max_Free format 9,999,999,999
set echo off

select a.tablespace_name,
(sum(a.tots)-sum(a.sumb))/sum(a.tots)*100 "% used"
from
(
select tablespace_name,0 tots,sum(bytes/1024/1024) sumb,
round(max(bytes/1024/1024),2) largest,count(*) chunks
from dba_free_space a
group by tablespace_name
union
select tablespace_name,sum(bytes/1024/1024) tots,0,0,0 from
dba_data_files
group by tablespace_name) a
group by a.tablespace_name
order by 7
/

title: AL Ref SW Query 8
description: Automation - sql script/query
code:
SELECT Round((df.bytes - SUM(fs.bytes)) * 100 / df.bytes) "% Used",
       df.tablespace_name "Tablespace"
  FROM dba_free_space fs,
       (SELECT tablespace_name,SUM(bytes) bytes
          FROM dba_data_files
         GROUP BY tablespace_name) df
 WHERE fs.tablespace_name (+)  = df.tablespace_name
       AND fs.tablespace_name = 'SYSTEM'
 GROUP BY df.tablespace_name,df.bytes

What you have looks good so far - my personal preference would be to also include ICMP nodes, as for most things you SHOULD be monitoring more than just latency and packet loss.
For the first point, I've found this is a pretty good query to look at devices that haven't had an uptime poll go through:
It basically checks to see if the system up-time poll date is greater than 3 statistic collection intervals ago. (I pulled this from an alert, but it should be fine for the report too)

SELECT Nodes.Caption, Nodes.NodeID FROM Nodes
WHERE
  status not in (2,9,11) AND  ObjectSubType <> 'ICMP'
 AND DATEDIFF(MINUTE, LastSystemUpTimePollUtc, getUTCdate()) > (StatCollection * 3)

title: Cisco Module
description: Cisco module operational status monitoring via custom pollers
code:
SELECT
    n.Caption AS NodeCaption,
    name.Status AS ModuleName,
    status.RawStatus AS ModuleStatus,

    CASE
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 3 THEN 'Minor'
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 4 THEN 'Major'
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 5 THEN 'Critical'
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 6 THEN 'Shutdown'
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 8 THEN 'Not Functioning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS StatusText,

    CASE
        WHEN status.RawStatus = 3 THEN '#FFC000'              -- Yellow
        WHEN status.RawStatus IN (4,5,6,8) THEN '#C00000'     -- Red
        ELSE '#A6A6A6'
    END AS Color

FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular AS name

JOIN (
    SELECT
        NodeID,
        CompressedRowID,
        MAX(DateTime) AS LastPoll
    FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular
    WHERE AssignmentName LIKE 'cefcModuleOperStatus%'
    GROUP BY NodeID, CompressedRowID
) AS latest
    ON latest.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND latest.CompressedRowID = name.CompressedRowID
    AND latest.LastPoll = name.DateTime

JOIN Orion.Nodes AS n
    ON n.NodeID = name.NodeID

LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular AS status
    ON status.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND status.RowID = name.RowID
    AND status.DateTime = name.DateTime
    AND status.AssignmentName LIKE 'cefcModuleOperStatus%'

WHERE
    name.AssignmentName LIKE 'entPhysicalName%'
    AND status.RawStatus IS NOT NULL
    AND status.RawStatus <> 2   -- 🔥 exclude OK

ORDER BY
    n.Caption,
    name.RowID

description: Cisco module operational status monitoring via custom pollers

SELECT

    CASE status.RawStatus
        WHEN 1 THEN 'Unknown'
        WHEN 2 THEN 'OK'
        WHEN 3 THEN 'Disabled'
        WHEN 5 THEN 'Booting'
        WHEN 6 THEN 'Self Test'
        WHEN 7 THEN 'Failed'
        WHEN 8 THEN 'Missing'
        WHEN 15 THEN 'Powered Down'
        WHEN 16 THEN 'Powered Up'
        ELSE CONCAT('Other (', status.RawStatus, ')')
    END AS StatusText,

    CASE status.RawStatus
        WHEN 2 THEN '#00B050'      -- Green (Healthy)
        WHEN 3 THEN '#A6A6A6'      -- Grey (Admin disabled)
        WHEN 5 THEN '#7030A0'      -- Purple (Booting)
        WHEN 6 THEN '#00B0F0'      -- Blue (Self test)
        WHEN 7 THEN '#C00000'      -- Red (Failed)
        WHEN 8 THEN '#D9D9D9'      -- Light Grey (Missing)
        WHEN 15 THEN '#FF8000'     -- Orange (Powered Down)
        WHEN 16 THEN '#FFC000'     -- Yellow (Powered Up)
        ELSE '#000000'
    END AS Color

FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular AS name

JOIN (
    SELECT
        NodeID,
        CompressedRowID,
        MAX(DateTime) AS LastPoll
    FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular
    WHERE AssignmentName LIKE 'cefcModuleOperStatus%'
    GROUP BY NodeID, CompressedRowID
) AS latest
    ON latest.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND latest.CompressedRowID = name.CompressedRowID
    AND latest.LastPoll = name.DateTime

LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular AS status
    ON status.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND status.RowID = name.RowID
    AND status.DateTime = name.DateTime
    AND status.AssignmentName LIKE 'cefcModuleOperStatus%'

title: SQL CP P VS C
description: Custom Pollers - sql script/query
code:
WITH CTE AS (
    SELECT
        n.Caption,
        cp.UniqueName,
        cps.DateTime AS Per_Poll_Time,
        cps.Status AS Pre_State,
        s.DateTime AS Last_Poll,
        s.Status AS Cur_State,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cps.CustomPollerAssignmentID ORDER BY cps.DateTime DESC) AS rn
    FROM CustomPollerStatistics cps
    INNER JOIN CustomPollerAssignment A ON cps.CustomPollerAssignmentID = A.CustomPollerAssignmentID
    INNER JOIN Nodes n ON A.NodeID = n.NodeID
    Inner JOIN CustomPollerStatus s ON s.CustomPollerAssignmentID = A.CustomPollerAssignmentID
    INNER JOIN CustomPollers cp ON A.CustomPollerID = cp.CustomPollerID
    WHERE cps.DateTime < DATEADD(MINUTE, -10, GETUTCDATE())
)
SELECT
    Caption,
    UniqueName,
    Per_Poll_Time,
    Pre_State,
    Last_Poll,
    Cur_State
FROM CTE
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY Caption;

title: DB Latency Part 1
description: SQL Server database I/O latency analysis for SolarWinds DB performance
code:
SELECT  DB_NAME(vfs.database_id) AS database_name ,physical_name AS [Physical Name],
       size_on_disk_bytes / 1024 / 1024. AS [Size of Disk] ,
       CAST(io_stall_read_ms/(1.0 + num_of_reads) AS NUMERIC(10,1)) AS [Average Read latency] ,
       CAST(io_stall_write_ms/(1.0 + num_of_writes) AS NUMERIC(10,1)) AS [Average Write latency] ,
       CAST((io_stall_read_ms + io_stall_write_ms)
/(1.0 + num_of_reads + num_of_writes)
AS NUMERIC(10,1)) AS [Average Total Latency],
       num_of_bytes_read / NULLIF(num_of_reads, 0) AS    [Average Bytes Per Read],
       num_of_bytes_written / NULLIF(num_of_writes, 0) AS   [Average Bytes Per Write]
FROM    sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) AS vfs
 JOIN sys.master_files AS mf
   ON vfs.database_id = mf.database_id AND vfs.file_id = mf.file_id
ORDER BY [Average Total Latency] DESC

title: DB Latency Part 2
description: SQL Server database I/O latency analysis for SolarWinds DB performance
code:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SELECT  DB_NAME(a.database_id) AS [Database Name] ,
       b.name + N' [' + b.type_desc COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS + N']' AS [Logical File Name] ,
       UPPER(SUBSTRING(b.physical_name, 1, 2)) AS [Drive] ,
       CAST(( ( a.size_on_disk_bytes / 1024.0 ) / (1024.0*1024.0) ) AS DECIMAL(9,2)) AS [Size (GB)] ,
       a.io_stall_read_ms AS [Total IO Read Stall] ,
       a.num_of_reads AS [Total Reads] ,
       CASE WHEN a.num_of_bytes_read > 0
           THEN CAST(a.num_of_bytes_read/1024.0/1024.0/1024.0 AS NUMERIC(23,1))
           ELSE 0
       END AS [GB Read],
       CAST(a.io_stall_read_ms / ( 1.0 * a.num_of_reads ) AS INT) AS [Avg Read Stall (ms)] ,
       CASE
           WHEN b.type = 0 THEN 30 /* data files */
           WHEN b.type = 1 THEN 5 /* log files */
           ELSE 0
       END AS [Max Rec Read Stall Avg],
       a.io_stall_write_ms AS [Total IO Write Stall] ,
       a.num_of_writes [Total Writes] ,
       CASE WHEN a.num_of_bytes_written > 0
           THEN CAST(a.num_of_bytes_written/1024.0/1024.0/1024.0 AS NUMERIC(23,1))
           ELSE 0
       END AS [GB Written],
       CAST(a.io_stall_write_ms / ( 1.0 * a.num_of_writes ) AS INT) AS [Avg Write Stall (ms)] ,
       CASE
           WHEN b.type = 0 THEN 30 /* data files */
           WHEN b.type = 1 THEN 2 /* log files */
           ELSE 0
       END AS [Max Rec Write Stall Avg] ,
       b.physical_name AS [Physical File Name],
       CASE
           WHEN b.name = 'tempdb' THEN 'N/A'
           WHEN b.type = 1 THEN 'N/A' /* log files */
           ELSE 'PAGEIOLATCH*'
       END AS [Read-Related Wait Stat],
       CASE
           WHEN b.type = 1 THEN 'WRITELOG' /* log files */
           WHEN b.name = 'tempdb' THEN 'xxx' /* tempdb data files */
           WHEN b.type = 0 THEN 'ASYNC_IO_COMPLETION' /* data files */
           ELSE 'xxx'
       END AS [Write-Related Wait Stat],
       GETDATE() AS [Sample Time],
       b.type_desc
FROM   sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) AS a
INNER JOIN sys.master_files AS b ON a.file_id = b.file_id
AND a.database_id = b.database_id
WHERE   a.num_of_reads > 0
AND a.num_of_writes > 0
ORDER BY CAST(a.io_stall_read_ms / ( 1.0 * a.num_of_reads ) AS INT) DESC;

description: FortiGate managed switch monitoring via custom pollers
code:
SELECT
    n.Caption AS FortiGate,
    n.DetailsURL AS Link,
    cps.CompressedRowID AS SwRowID,

    CASE
        WHEN C.Status = 200 THEN 'Active'
        WHEN C.Status = 100 THEN 'Passive'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS Priority,

    MAX(CASE
            WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceName%'
            THEN cps.Status
        END) AS SwName,

    MAX(CASE
            WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceIp%'
            THEN cps.Status
        END) AS SwIp,

    MAX(CASE
            WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwCpu%'
            THEN cps.RawStatus
        END) AS SwCpu,

    MAX(CASE
            WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwMemory%'
            THEN cps.RawStatus
        END) AS SwMemory,

    CASE
        WHEN MAX(CASE
                    WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceStatus%'
                    THEN cps.RawStatus
                 END) = 1 THEN 0
        WHEN MAX(CASE
                    WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceStatus%'
                    THEN cps.RawStatus
                 END) = 0 THEN 3
        ELSE 2
    END AS SwStatus

FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular cps

JOIN Orion.Nodes n
     ON n.NodeID = cps.NodeID

INNER JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNode C
        ON C.NodeID = n.NodeID
       AND C.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgHaPriority%'

WHERE
       cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSw%'
  AND cps.AssignmentName NOT LIKE 'fgSwPortName%'
  AND cps.AssignmentName NOT LIKE 'fgSwPortStatus%'

GROUP BY
    n.Caption,
    n.DetailsURL,
    cps.CompressedRowID,
    CASE
        WHEN C.Status = 200 THEN 'Active'
        WHEN C.Status = 100 THEN 'Passive'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END

ORDER BY
    n.Caption,
    cps.CompressedRowID;

        WHEN MAX(CASE
                    WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceStatus%'
                    THEN cps.RawStatus
                 END) = 1 THEN 0
        WHEN MAX(CASE
                    WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceStatus%'
                    THEN cps.RawStatus
                 END) = 0 THEN 3
        ELSE 2
    END AS SwStatus,

    MAX(cps.DateTime) AS LastPoll

FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular cps

JOIN Orion.Nodes n
    ON n.NodeID = cps.NodeID

JOIN (
    SELECT
        NodeID,
        CompressedRowID,
        MAX(DateTime) AS LastPoll
    FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular
    WHERE AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSw%'
      AND DateTime >= ADDHOUR(-1, GETUTCDATE())
    GROUP BY NodeID, CompressedRowID
) latest
    ON latest.NodeID = cps.NodeID
   AND latest.CompressedRowID = cps.CompressedRowID
   AND latest.LastPoll = cps.DateTime

WHERE
      cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSw%'
  AND cps.AssignmentName NOT LIKE 'fgSwPortName%'
  AND cps.AssignmentName NOT LIKE 'fgSwPortStatus%'

GROUP BY
    n.Caption,
    n.DetailsURL,
    cps.CompressedRowID

HAVING
    MAX(CASE
            WHEN cps.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgSwDeviceIp%'
            THEN cps.Status
        END) <> ''

ORDER BY
    n.Caption,
    cps.CompressedRowID;

title: FortiWAN LINK
description: FortiGate SD-WAN link health monitoring with latency, jitter, and packet loss
code:
SELECT
    n.Caption AS NodeCaption,
    -- Link Name
    name.Status AS LinkName,
    -- Link State (0=Down,1=Up)
    state.RawStatus AS LinkState,
    -- Latency (RawStatus)
    latency.RawStatus AS LatencyMs,
    -- Jitter (RawStatus)
    jitter.RawStatus AS JitterMs,
    -- Packet Loss (RawStatus)
    loss.RawStatus AS PacketLossPercent,
    -- Severity color for Modern Dashboard
    CASE
        WHEN state.RawStatus = 1 THEN 3  -- Green
        WHEN state.RawStatus = 0 THEN 0   -- Red
        ELSE 2                            -- Yellow
    END AS LinkSeverity,

    name.RowID

FROM Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular name
JOIN Orion.Nodes n ON n.NodeID = name.NodeID

-- State
LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular state
    ON state.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND state.RowID = name.RowID
    AND state.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgVWLHealthCheckLinkState%'

-- Latency
LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular latency
    ON latency.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND latency.RowID = name.RowID
    AND latency.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgVWLHealthCheckLinkLatency%'

-- Jitter
LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular jitter
    ON jitter.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND jitter.RowID = name.RowID
    AND jitter.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgVWLHealthCheckLinkJitter%'

-- Packet Loss
LEFT JOIN Orion.NPM.CustomPollerStatusOnNodeTabular loss
    ON loss.NodeID = name.NodeID
    AND loss.RowID = name.RowID
    AND loss.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgVWLHealthCheckLinkPacketLoss%'
WHERE
    name.AssignmentName LIKE 'fgVWLHealthCheckLinkName%'

ORDER BY
    n.Caption,
    name.RowID;

--Unmanaged
SELECT
    N.Caption AS [Node],
    DATETRUNC('minute', N.UnManageFrom) AS [Suppressed From],
    CONCAT(DayDiff(N.UnManageFrom,GetDate()), ' days') as [Suppressed days],
    CASE
        WHEN ToString(tolocal(N.UnManageUntil))='Jan  1 9999 12:00AM' THEN ' - Forever - '
        ELSE CONCAT(DayDiff(N.UnManageFrom,N.UnManageUntil), ' days')
    END as [Total Suppressed days],
    CASE
        WHEN ToString(tolocal(N.UnManageUntil))='Jan  1 9999 12:00AM' THEN ' - Forever - '
        ELSE CONCAT(DayDiff(GETDATE(),N.UnManageUntil), ' days')
    END as [Days until not Suppressed],
    AE.AccountID AS [Supressed By],
    'Unmanaged' AS [Type],
    CONCAT('/NetPerfMon/Images/Vendors/',N.Icon) AS [_ICONFor_Node],
    N.DetailsUrl AS [_LinkFor_Node]
    --CONCAT('/Orion/images/StatusIcons/',N.StatusIcon) as Icon
FROM Orion.Nodes N
LEFT OUTER JOIN (
        SELECT
            AE.NetObjectID,
            Max(AE.AuditEventID) as [AuditEventID]
        FROM Orion.AuditingEvents AE
        where ActionTypeID = 34 -- 'Orion.NodeUnmanaged'
        group by AE.NetObjectID
) AS [LastMuteEvents] ON LastMuteEvents.NetObjectID=N.NodeID
LEFT OUTER JOIN Orion.AuditingEvents AE ON AE.AuditEventID=LastMuteEvents.AuditEventid
WHERE
    N.UnManaged=1

title: PIVOT Multi UDP SAM Part 2
description: SWQL-approved PIVOT technique for transposing multi-statistic data to columns
code:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SELECT
     -- Node, Application, and Component names
     [Applications].Node.DisplayName AS [Node] -- <-- Use navigational properties to get "up" to node
     , [Applications].DisplayName AS [Application]
     , [Applications].Components.DisplayName AS [Component] -- <-- Use navigational properties to get "down" to component
     -- Node, Application, and Component links
     , [Applications].Node.DetailsUrl AS [_LinkFor_Node]
     , [Applications].DetailsUrl AS [_LinkFor_Application]
     , [Applications].Components.DetailsUrl AS [_LinkFor_Component]
     -- Node, Application, and Component status

     , CONCAT (
          '/Orion/StatusIcon.ashx?entity=Orion.Nodes&status='
          , [Applications].Node.STATUS
          ) AS [_IconFor_Node]
     , CONCAT (
          '/Orion/StatusIcon.ashx?entity=Orion.APM.Application&status='
          , [Applications].STATUS
          ) AS [_IconFor_Application]
     , CONCAT (
          '/Orion/StatusIcon.ashx?entity=Orion.APM.Component&status='
          , [Applications].Components.STATUS
          ) AS [_IconFor_Component]
     -- Since the Orion.APM.MultipleStatisticData entity has both a NumericalData and a StringData property for each record, we can choose between the two for the output
     -- This pulls the string that's been stored for the component ID with Unique ID 'AccountName'
     , [MSData_Account].StringData AS [AccountName]
     -- This pulls the numerical value that's been stored for the component ID with Unique ID 'DaysUntilPasswordExpiration'
     , [MSData_PwdExpiry].NumericData AS [DaysUntilExpiration]
     -- This is just some clever math to get the expiration date
     --   1.                                                   Get the Current Date
     --   2.                         Add X days to the current date
     --   3. Truncate that date to stop at "day" - no hours, minutes, seconds
     , DATETRUNC('day', ADDDAY([MSData_PwdExpiry].NumericData, GETUTCDATE())) AS [ExpirationDate]
     -- This pulls the numerical value that's been stored for the component ID with Unique ID 'LockoutStatus'
     , CASE
          WHEN [MSData_Lockout].NumericData = 1
               THEN 'Locked Out'
          ELSE ''
          END AS [LockoutStatus]
-- We'll start our link with the application since we'll be filtering on one of it's elements below
FROM Orion.APM.Application AS [Applications]

-- We only need one row from the Orion.APM.MultipleStatisticData table, but it must match the component ID and specific names
LEFT JOIN Orion.APM.MultipleStatisticData AS [MSData_Account] -- this is the alias for the account name
     ON [Applications].Components.ComponentID = [MSData_Account].ComponentID
          AND [MSData_Account].Name = 'AccountName' -- <-- this is the "Unique ID" listed in the component definition

LEFT JOIN Orion.APM.MultipleStatisticData AS [MSData_PwdExpiry] -- this is the alias for the password expiration date
     ON [Applications].Components.ComponentID = [MSData_PwdExpiry].ComponentID
          AND [MSData_PwdExpiry].Name = 'DaysUntilPasswordExpiration' -- <-- this is the "Unique ID" listed in the component definition

LEFT JOIN Orion.APM.MultipleStatisticData AS [MSData_Lockout] -- this is the alias for the lockout status
     ON [Applications].Components.ComponentID = [MSData_Lockout].ComponentID
          AND [MSData_Lockout].Name = 'LockoutStatus' -- <-- this is the "Unique ID" listed in the component definition

WHERE -- This is where you put the application name you want to filter on
     [Applications].Name = 'Local User Account Status (Windows)'

code:
SELECT
TOLOCAL(ST.EventTime) AS [Start Time]
,TOLOCAL(ET.EventTime) AS [Finish Time]
,CONCAT((CASE WHEN (SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime,GETUTCDATE()))/60/60) >= 1 THEN CONCAT(SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime,GETUTCDATE()))/60/60%24, 'h ') ELSE '' END)
, (CASE WHEN (SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime, GETUTCDATE()))/60) >= 1 THEN CONCAT(SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime,GETUTCDATE()))/60%60, 'm ') ELSE '' END)
,(CASE WHEN (SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime, GETUTCDATE()))) >= 1
THEN CONCAT(SECONDDIFF(ST.EventTime,ISNULL(ET.EventTime,GETUTCDATE()))%60, 's ') ELSE '' END)) AS [Duration]
FROM Orion.Events ST
LEFT JOIN Orion.Events ET ON ET.NetObjectID = ST.NetObjectID AND ET.EventTime = (SELECT TOP 1 x.EventTime FROM Orion.Events x WHERE x.EventTime >= ST.EventTime AND x.EventType = 5101 AND x.Message = 'Nightly Maintenance Completed' AND x.NetObjectID = ST.NetObjectID ORDER BY x.EventTime)
WHERE ST.EventType = '5100' AND ST.Message = 'Starting Nightly Maintenance' AND ST.EventTime > ADDDAY(-7,GETUTCDATE())
ORDER BY ST.EventTime DESC

title: Oracle DB size
description: SWQL dashboard query for SolarWinds Orion
code:
Oracle DB size:
SELECT n.Caption AS Node,a.Name AS DB,c.ComponentName AS Metric, ce.AvgStatisticData AS Data_File_GB
FROM Orion.APM.Component(nolock=true) c
JOIN Orion.APM.CurrentComponentStatus(nolock=true) ccs ON c.ComponentID = ccs.ComponentID
JOIN Orion.APM.ChartEvidence(nolock=true) ce ON ce.ComponentStatusID = ccs.ComponentStatusID
JOIN Orion.APM.Application(nolock=true) a ON c.ApplicationID = a.ApplicationID
JOIN Orion.Nodes(nolock=true) n ON a.NodeID = n.NodeID
WHERE c.ComponentName = 'Data files size (GB)'
